optionList.content: Using an electric razor for shaving
Rubbing the client’s skin after shaving
Washing the client’s face with hot water after shaving
Using shaving cream which contains numbing agents
Using an electric razor for shaving
Rubbing the client’s skin after shaving
Washing the client’s face with hot water after shaving
Using shaving cream which contains numbing agents

studentQuestionArray.content: Which nursing intervention is beneficial when the nurse is preparing to shave a client who has neutropenia?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Neutropenia indicates that the client has a weaker defensive mechanism and is at a higher risk of infection. Razor scratches cause breaks in the skin integrity and increase the risk of entry for pathogens. Therefore, an electric razor is preferred for the client.
Option 2:Rubbing the client’s skin after shaving may cause irritation.
Option 3:Cool water is used for washing the client’s face after shaving. It helps to close the skin pores.
Option 4:Use of shaving creams, which contain numbing agents, should be avoided because it closes the pores and stiffens the beard.

optionList.content: Providing oral cleansing and subglottic suctioning once a day
Applying water-soluble lip moisturizer
Applying a 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice daily
Using normal saline solution for oral rinses
Providing oral cleansing and subglottic suctioning once a day
Applying water-soluble lip moisturizer
Applying a 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice daily
Using normal saline solution for oral rinses

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action made by the nurse while providing oral care to a client who is receiving mechanical ventilation may cause complications in the client?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should provide oral cleansing and subglottic suctioning to the client at least every 2 hours to keep the oral cavity clean and clear of secretions. This action reduces the risk of ventilator-associated pneumonia.
Option 2:Applying water-soluble lip moisturizer prevents dryness of the lips.
Option 3:Applying a 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice daily reduces risk of infection.
Option 4:Using normal saline solution for oral rinses reduces risk of microbial infection.

optionList.content: “I should wipe the perineal area from back to front after bowel movements.”
“I should wear loose clothing on my lower body.”
“I should allow air flow by wearing cotton underwear.”
“I should take a warm bath for 10 to 15 minutes in clear water.”
“I should wipe the perineal area from back to front after bowel movements.”
“I should wear loose clothing on my lower body.”
“I should allow air flow by wearing cotton underwear.”
“I should take a warm bath for 10 to 15 minutes in clear water.”

studentQuestionArray.content: Which statement made by the client who has vulvovaginitis indicates a need for further teaching about vaginal hygiene?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Wiping the perianal area from front to back after each bowel movement prevents infection. Wiping perianal area from back-to-front increases the risk of infection.
Option 2:Wearing loose clothing on the lower body prevents skin irritation and promotes ventilation.
Option 3:Allowing air flow by wearing cotton underwear helps to dry the perianal area. Moisture in this area increases the risk of infection.
Option 4:Taking a warm bath for 10 to 15 minutes in clear water removes dirt.

optionList.content: A client who has diabetes
A client who has malnutrition
A client who has hypertension
A client who has musculoskeletal injury
A client who has diabetes
A client who has malnutrition
A client who has hypertension
A client who has musculoskeletal injury

studentQuestionArray.content: Which client may require a prescription for nail trimming?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Diabetes causes poor blood circulation in the lower extremities. Poor blood circulation coupled with diabetic neuropathy may cause decreased sensation in the extremities. As a result, nail trimming may lead to trauma without the client knowing it. In addition, the wounds may take a longer time to heal. Therefore, a prescription is required to trim the nails for a diabetic client.
Option 2:Malnutrition does not cause any complications associated with nail trimming. Nail trimming is safe for a client with malnutrition.
Option 3:Nail trimming can be performed in a hypertensive client because it does not cause complications.
Option 4:A back rub is contraindicated in a client who has a musculoskeletal injury. Nail trimming can safely be performed on the client, however.

Test Taking Tips: Consider which health issue could cause trauma to the client without the client knowing it.

optionList.content: Applying deodorant to the client
Disconnecting the IV tubing during the bath
Folding the washcloth around the nurse’s hand to make a mitt, tucking in loose corners
Using a different corner of the washcloth (without soap) to wipe each eyelid
Applying deodorant to the client
Disconnecting the IV tubing during the bath
Folding the washcloth around the nurse’s hand to make a mitt, tucking in loose corners
Using a different corner of the washcloth (without soap) to wipe each eyelid

studentQuestionArray.content: The registered nurse instructs the student nurse to provide a complete bed bath to a client who has an intravenous (IV) line. Which action made by the student nurse needs to be corrected by the registered nurse?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Deodorants can be applied if the client wishes to because it provides a feeling of well-being.
Option 2:The IV tubing should not be disconnected during the bath because it may cause a break in the sterile system and increase the risk of infection.
Option 3:The nurse should fold the washcloth around the nurse’s hand to make a mitt because loose ends of cloth cool quickly and the client may feel uncomfortable.
Option 4:The nurse should use a different corner of the washcloth (without soap) to wipe each eyelid because soap may cause irritation of eyes.

Test Taking Tips: Read the answer choices carefully and consider which one could create a portal of entry for pathogens into the client.

optionList.content: To help the client relax
To improve circulation
To prevent pressure sores
To provide chest physiotherapy
To help the client relax
To improve circulation
To prevent pressure sores
To provide chest physiotherapy

studentQuestionArray.content: What could be the reason for including a back massage at H.S. (hour of sleep)?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:If a back massage is given during H.S. care, it helps the client relax and promotes sleep.
Option 2:A back massage helps to improve circulation. However, to achieve this purpose, the back massage need not be provided before sleep.
Option 3:A back massage helps to improve circulation and prevent pressure sores. However, the purpose can be achieved by repositioning the client hourly during the day.
Option 4:A back massage helps to provide chest physiotherapy. However, nighttime is not an appropriate time because the client may want to sleep.

optionList.content: Apply 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice a day.
Rinse the oral cavity using solutions containing lidocaine.
Use a bland rinse of diluted salt and sodium bicarbonate.
Use an antifungal agent or a multiagent rinse.
Apply 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice a day.
Rinse the oral cavity using solutions containing lidocaine.
Use a bland rinse of diluted salt and sodium bicarbonate.
Use an antifungal agent or a multiagent rinse.

studentQuestionArray.content: How will the nurse provide oral hygiene to a frail older adult who is on a ventilator and has undergone cardiac surgery?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should use 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate solution twice a day for effective oral hygiene.
Option 2:Rinsing the oral cavity using solutions containing lidocaine is useful in clients receiving chemotherapy.
Option 3:A bland rinse of diluted salt and sodium bicarbonate is used in preventing mucositis or yeast colonization induced by chemotherapy or radiation therapy.
Option 4:Using an antifungal agent or a multiagent rinse is useful in clients receiving chemotherapy.

optionList.content: Tongue
Sclera
Buccal mucosa
Palms
Tongue
Sclera
Buccal mucosa
Palms

studentQuestionArray.content: Which area of the body should the nurse inspect to assess jaundice?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The tongue is an appropriate area to assess for cyanosis in dark-skinned clients.
Option 2:The sclera is the best place to assess deposition of bile pigments in jaundice.
Option 3:Cyanosis can be assessed by inspecting the buccal mucosa.
Option 4:Cyanosis can be assessed by inspecting the palms.

optionList.content: Clean the perineum from front to back, using a clean portion of the washcloth.
Clean the perineum in a circular motion, using a clean portion of the washcloth.
Clean the perineum using a spray, no-rinse cleanser or soft wipes.
Clean the perineum using warm water and place a bedpan beneath the perineum.
Clean the perineum from front to back, using a clean portion of the washcloth.
Clean the perineum in a circular motion, using a clean portion of the washcloth.
Clean the perineum using a spray, no-rinse cleanser or soft wipes.
Clean the perineum using warm water and place a bedpan beneath the perineum.

studentQuestionArray.content: What intervention should the nurse provide to decrease the risk of developing a urinary tract infection while providing perineal care to a female client?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:During perineal care, fecal particles may cause skin breakdown due to the enzyme activity of E. coli. Cleaning the perineum from front to back using a clean portion of a washcloth helps in preventing a urinary tract infection.
Option 2:Cleaning the perineum using a clean portion of the washcloth in a circular motion is effective in male clients.
Option 3:Cleaning the perineum using a spray, no-rinse cleanser, or soft wipes is effective if the client is unable to control her bowels.
Option 4:Cleaning the perineum using warm water and placing a bedpan beneath the perineum may provide comfort but may not aid in preventing a urinary tract infection.

optionList.content: Cheilosis
Glossitis
Stomatitis
Periodontal disease
Cheilosis
Glossitis
Stomatitis
Periodontal disease

studentQuestionArray.content: Which complication will occur in the client if gingivitis is left untreated?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Cheilosis occurs due to a vitamin B–complex deficiency.
Option 2:Glossitis occurs due to a vitamin B12, folic acid, and iron deficiency.
Option 3:Stomatitis occurs due to mechanical trauma, irritants, nutritional deficiencies, and systemic infection.
Option 4:If gingivitis is left untreated, it may cause periodontal disease.

optionList.content: Stop the procedure immediately
Discuss the finding with the client
Notify the primary health-care provider
Leave the client in a comfortable position
Stop the procedure immediately
Discuss the finding with the client
Notify the primary health-care provider
Leave the client in a comfortable position

studentQuestionArray.content: While shampooing a client’s hair, the nurse finds nits in the client’s hair. What should be the first nursing action in this situation?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should immediately stop the procedure if the nurse finds nits in the client’s hair.
Option 2:After stopping the procedure, the nurse should discuss the finding with the client and offer reassurance.
Option 3:Notifying the primary health-care provider and obtaining a prescription for the desired shampoo are secondary interventions.
Option 4:The nurse should first stop the procedure, and then may leave the client in a comfortable position until a prescription is obtained for a prescriptive shampoo.

Test Taking Tips: Look for words such as ‘most important’, ‘best’, ‘priority’, and ‘first’ in question stems. These trigger words tell you that multiple answers may be partially correct, but you’ll be looking for the nursing intervention that should be done before anything else.

Disinfectant

studentQuestionArray.content: Which solvent is used for cleaning an artificial eye?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Alcohol may cause irritation of the eye socket and damage the artificial eye.
Option 2:Disinfectants may irritate the eye socket and damage the artificial eye.
Option 3:A saline solution should be used for cleaning the artificial eye because it does not harm the prosthesis.
Option 4:Strong solvents may cause irritation of the socket and may damage the artificial eye.

optionList.content: Applying foot powder regularly
Soaking feet for 5 to 20 minutes
Cleaning the feet with no-rinse soaps
Applying moisturizing lotion on both the tops and bottoms of the feet
Applying foot powder regularly
Soaking feet for 5 to 20 minutes
Cleaning the feet with no-rinse soaps
Applying moisturizing lotion on both the tops and bottoms of the feet

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action made by the client with diabetic neuropathy when performing foot care may increase the risk of complications?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Foot powder absorbs moisture and functions as a nonirritating deodorant. It helps to keep the feet dry and prevents skin maceration.
Option 2:The client who has diabetes should not soak his or her feet because it may remove natural oils and cause cracking of the skin. It also increases the risk of burns, even if the water is at the recommended temperature.
Option 3:No-rinse soaps remove loose debris and prevent drying of the skin.
Option 4:Moisturizing lotion hydrates the skin and helps prevent dryness of the skin.

Test Taking Tips: Consider which action made by the diabetic client could remove an essential element of moisture from his or her skin.

optionList.content: Calluses
Tinea pedis
Hallux valgus
Plantar warts
Calluses
Tinea pedis
Hallux valgus
Plantar warts

studentQuestionArray.content: Which complication occurs in the feet and manifests as painful growths caused by a virus?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Calluses are bony prominences in the weight-bearing part of the foot such as plantar, soles, and surfaces of the feet and the heels.
Option 2:Tinea pedis is a fungal infection that is occurs because an accumulation of moisture.
Option 3:Hallux valgus is an enlargement of the first metatarsal joint at the base of the great toe and it progresses to leaning of the big toe. It occurs due to use of tight-fitting shoes or a disease like arthritis.
Option 4:Plantar warts may be caused by a virus and cause painful growths. They may occur on any part of the sole of the foot. They often develop under pressure points like the heel or the ball of the foot.

optionList.content: Dryness of the mouth
Numbness of the oral tissue
Inflammation of the tongue
Excessive growth of gingival cells
Dryness of the mouth
Numbness of the oral tissue
Inflammation of the tongue
Excessive growth of gingival cells

studentQuestionArray.content: Which adverse effect associated with diuretic use does the nurse expect to find in the client when providing oral care?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Diuretics cause dryness of mouth by facilitating fluid loss.
Option 2:Numbness of the tissue in the oral cavity is a sign of oral malignancy.
Option 3:Inflammation of the tongue is a sign of glossitis, and can be caused by vitamin B12, folic acid, and iron deficiencies.
Option 4:Excessive growth of the gingival cells (gingival hyperplasia) can be caused by phenytoin (Dilantin).

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is teaching general hygiene to a client who has bilateral hearing impairment. The nurse concludes that the client has a high risk of trauma to the ear. Which finding enabled the nurse to make such a conclusion?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Attempting to force a hearing aid into the wrong ear may cause trauma to the ear.
Option 2:Wet hearing aids may cause corrosion of the battery.
Option 3:Handling the hearing aid by the edges using the thumb and forefinger prevents whistling during insertion.
Option 4:Holding the hearing aid in the dominant hand and using the nondominant hand to gently pull the ear up and back helps to open the ear canal slightly for easier insertion and a better fit.

The skin becomes pale.
The skin becomes wrinkled.
The skin becomes unevenly pigmented.
The skin becomes dry, scaly, and itchy.

studentQuestionArray.content: What happens when the collagen and the elastin fibers in the dermis weaken and become less elastic?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The skin may become pale if the vascularity of the skin reduces.
Option 2:The skin becomes wrinkled if collagen and elastin fibers become weak and lose elasticity.
Option 3:The skin becomes unevenly pigmented if the numbers of melanocytes decrease.
Option 4:The skin may become dry, scaly, and itchy, if the sebaceous and sweat glands do not function optimally.

Position the client in a semi-Fowler’s position.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which precaution should the nurse follow to prevent aspiration while brushing the client’s teeth?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The supine position is preferred while providing a complete bed bath to the client. Placing the client in a supine position increases the risk of aspiration.
Option 2:The prone position is suitable for a client while washing the back and buttocks.
Option 3:Placing the client in a high-Fowler’s position prevents aspiration and makes the procedure easier.
Option 4:The semi-Fowler’s position is suitable while providing foot care to the client. It is easy to provide foot care to the client in this position.

optionList.content: By using gauze squares
By using a small toothbrush
By using a foam mouth swab
By using lemon glycerin swabs
By using normal saline mouth wash
By using gauze squares
By using a small toothbrush
By using a foam mouth swab
By using lemon glycerin swabs
By using normal saline mouth wash

studentQuestionArray.content: How does the nurse ensure safety while providing oral care to a client who has thrombocytopenia? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The client has a high risk of gum bleeding due to thrombocytopenia. Use of gauze squares may increase the risk of bleeding because of friction against the delicate surfaces of the gums.
Option 2:A toothbrush increases the chance of gum bleeding; therefore, a toothbrush should not be used for this client.
Option 3:A foam mouth swab does not cause gum bleeding; therefore, it is recommended for the client.
Option 4:Lemon glycerin swabs increase risk of gum bleeding and decalcification of tooth enamel; therefore, they are not recommended for the client.
Option 5:Normal saline mouthwash is advisable for the client because it does not cause gum bleeding.

Test Taking Tips: Recall which agents might cause or increase gum bleeding.

optionList.content: Trimming the nails with a nail clipper
Cutting the lateral edges of nails deeply
Removing hangnails with cuticle clipper
Applying a moisturizing lotion on the nails
Cleaning under the nails with sharp instruments
Trimming the nails with a nail clipper
Cutting the lateral edges of nails deeply
Removing hangnails with cuticle clipper
Applying a moisturizing lotion on the nails
Cleaning under the nails with sharp instruments

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action during nail care may result in complications for a client who is diagnosed with peripheral vascular disease? Select All That Apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Peripheral vascular disease impairs the circulation. Therefore, the nails should not be cut with a nail clipper; filing the nails is preferred.
Option 2:The lateral edges of the nails should be slightly rounded to prevent scratching. They should not be cut deeply because it can lead to injury.
Option 3:Hangnails should be removed with a cuticle clipper to facilitate safe removal without injury.
Option 4:A moisturizing lotion applied on the nails helps to soften the cuticles.
Option 5:Cleaning under the nails with blunt instruments helps prevent injury.
